metadata.title: Important Days and Events Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: International Red Panda Day 2025 is celebrated on which day?
questions.explanation: International Red Panda Day 2025 is observed on 20th September, the third Saturday of September. The day focuses on creating awareness about red pandas, an endangered species. It was started in 2010 by the Red Panda Network, a Nepal-based non-profit organisation. The aim is to dedicate one day every year for global awareness and conservation action. Red pandas face threats from shrinking forests, poaching, and illegal pet trade. Their population is now less than 10,000 in the wild. Awareness campaigns help raise funds, educate children, and support forest communities in conservation efforts.

questions.question: The International Literature Festival “Unmesh” was recently inaugurated in which city?
questions.explanation: The third edition of the International Literary Festival ‘Unmesh’ was inaugurated at Gyan Bhawan, Patna on 26 September. The festival is organised by the Ministry of Culture and Sahitya Akademi and will continue until 28th September 2025. More than 550 participants from 16 countries representing 100+ languages are attending. Bihar was highlighted as the birthplace of literary and spiritual luminaries, with monuments of compassion enduring through time.

questions.question: Where was Uttar Pradesh International Trade Show-2025 (UPITS-2025) inaugurated?
questions.explanation: The Uttar Pradesh International Trade Show-2025 (UPITS-2025) was inaugurated at Greater Noida, Gautam Buddha Nagar district. The event runs from 25th to 29th September 2025 with Russia as the partner country. The trade show aligns with Make in India, Vocal for Local, and Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiatives. It aims to attract investors, generate fresh investments, create employment, and enhance Uttar Pradesh’s industrial profile. The 2025 edition is larger than previous ones, showcasing the state’s industrial strength and investment potential.

questions.question: World Pharmacists Day is celebrated globally on which day every year?
questions.explanation: World Pharmacists Day is celebrated globally on 25th September every year since 2009. It marks the anniversary of the International Pharmaceutical Federation (FIP), established in 1912. The day recognizes the vital role of pharmacists in healthcare systems worldwide. It promotes awareness of pharmacists’ contributions to medication safety, patient care, and overall health improvement. The 2025 theme is “Think Health, Think Pharmacist,” emphasizing pharmacists as key healthcare providers.

questions.question: Where was the the sixth edition of River Festival held?
questions.explanation: The Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts (IGNCA) under the Ministry of Culture hosted the sixth edition of Nadi Utsav from 25th to 27th September 2025 at its Janpath premises, New Delhi. The festival celebrated rivers as lifelines of ecology and culture. It was inaugurated on 25th September by Union Jal Shakti Minister. The event featured seminars, films, performances, and exhibitions highlighting rivers’ ecological and cultural significance.

questions.question: What is the theme of World Tourism Day 2025?
questions.explanation: World Tourism Day is observed annually on 27 September to highlight tourism’s role in cultural exchange, economic growth, and global connectivity. It is an initiative of the United Nations World Tourism Organisation (UNWTO), first celebrated in 1980. The day commemorates the adoption of the UNWTO statutes in 1970. The theme for 2025 is “Tourism and Sustainable Transformation”, focusing on tourism’s power to drive positive change. Malaysia is the host of World Tourism Day 2025 and the World Tourism Conference (WTC) in Melaka from 27–29 September.

questions.question: World Heart Day is observed every year on which day?
questions.explanation: World Heart Day is observed every year on September 29. The day raises awareness about heart diseases and preventive measures to manage cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) globally. The day promotes healthy lifestyles to prevent and control heart-related ailments. The theme for World Heart Day 2025 is “Don’t Miss a Beat”. It stresses continuous vigilance in heart health through healthy habits, regular check-ups, timely medical attention, and awareness of warning signs. The goal is to reduce deaths caused by cardiovascular diseases worldwide.

questions.question: International Day of Older Persons is observed on which day every year?
questions.explanation: International Day of Older Persons is observed on 1 October every year. On this day in 2025, HelpAge India, a Non-Governmental Organisation (NGO), launched the campaign ‘AdvantAge60: Powering Aspirations – Active, Enabled and Empowered’. The campaign aims to challenge ageing stereotypes and promote active, purposeful life after 60 years. A 2022 HelpAge report showed 40% of elders want to continue working and contributing. A 2025 study revealed youth see elders as dependent (48%), but also wise (51%) and respected (43%).

questions.question: What is the theme of World Space Week 2025?
questions.explanation: The United Nations (UN)’s World Space Week (WSW) is observed globally every year from October 4–10. World Space Week promotes global unity and highlights collaboration between space technology and climate science. The 2025 theme is “Living in Space”. This year’s theme focuses on humanity’s efforts to live in space, including new technologies, challenges, and teamwork.

questions.question: National Post Day is celebrated in India every year on which day?
questions.explanation: National Post Day is celebrated in India every year on October 10. It honors the Indian Postal Department for its vital role in India’s development and in connecting people nationwide. The Indian Postal Department, also known as India Post, was established in 1854 by Lord Dalhousie during the British era. It operates under the Department of Posts, which comes under the Ministry of Communications. India Post is the largest postal network in the world, serving even remote rural areas.
